channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: French government seeking release of 86-year-old French widow detained by ICE
item.description: The French government is pressing the U.S. Department of Homeland Security to release the 86-year-old French widow of a military veteran from immigration custody in Louisiana after she was detained earlier this month.
item.content:encoded:
The French government is pressing the U.S. Department of Homeland Security to release the 86-year-old French widow of a military veteran from immigration custody in Louisiana after she was detained earlier this month.

(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Bukele signs reforms allowing life prison sentences for people as young as 12
item.description: The reforms signed by Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele would apply to people convicted of committing or being an accomplice to crimes including homicide, femicide, rape and gang membership.
item.content:encoded:
The reforms signed by Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele would apply to people convicted of committing or being an accomplice to crimes including homicide, femicide, rape and gang membership.

(Image credit: Salvador Melendez)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Popes have spoken out on politics before. But with Trump and Pope Leo it's different
item.description: President Trump's attacks on Pope Leo are unprecedented, religious experts told NPR. Here's how the situation differs from other popes' political critiques.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's attacks on Pope Leo are unprecedented, religious experts told NPR. Here's how the situation differs from other popes' political critiques.

(Image credit: Alberto Pizzoli)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Greetings from Nairobi, where taking a matatu is no ordinary bus ride
item.description: The moment you board, the music grabs you. These privately owned, brightly painted minibuses are moving canvases, mobile sound systems — rolling declarations of what young Nairobi finds cool.
item.content:encoded: The moment you board, the music grabs you. These privately owned, brightly painted minibuses are moving canvases, mobile sound systems — rolling declarations of what young Nairobi finds cool.
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Igunza

item.title: Trump says new talks with Iran will happen soon. And, Eric Swalwell faces new allegations
item.description: President Trump says new talks with Iran could happen in the next two days. And, Democrat Eric Swalwell faces new allegations as a second woman comes forward accusing him of rape.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump says new talks with Iran could happen in the next two days. And, Democrat Eric Swalwell faces new allegations as a second woman comes forward accusing him of rape.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Trump says Iran talks could resume in days as U.S. military blocks Iranian ports
item.description: President Trump said a second round of direct U.S.-Iran peace talks could resume in Pakistan within the next two days, even as he instituted a naval blockade of all Iranian ports.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said a second round of direct U.S.-Iran peace talks could resume in Pakistan within the next two days, even as he instituted a naval blockade of all Iranian ports.

(Image credit: Jalaa Marey)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Fela Kuti is the first African artist to enter the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
item.description: In December, the late Nigerian superstar became the first African musician to get a Grammy lifetime achievement award. Now he's making history as well at the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame.
item.content:encoded:
In December, the late Nigerian superstar became the first African musician to get a Grammy lifetime achievement award. Now he's making history as well at the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame.

(Image credit: Leni Sinclair/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Ian Brennan

item.title: Pope heads to Cameroon as separatists announce 3-day pause in fighting
item.description: Pope Leo XIV is heading to the central African nation of Cameroon with a message of peace for its separatist region and for talks with President Paul Biya.
item.content:encoded:
Pope Leo XIV is heading to the central African nation of Cameroon with a message of peace for its separatist region and for talks with President Paul Biya.

(Image credit: Andrew Medichini)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: 'Humiliated, broken, powerless': Sudan enters fourth year of war
item.description: While parts of Sudan's capital show fragile signs of life, across the country the conflict between the army and a rival paramilitary continues to drive mass displacement, hunger and allegations of atrocities.
item.content:encoded:
While parts of Sudan's capital show fragile signs of life, across the country the conflict between the army and a rival paramilitary continues to drive mass displacement, hunger and allegations of atrocities.

(Image credit: Dan Kitwood)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Akinwotu

item.title: As fuel prices continue to rise, Iran war drives Europe toward new crisis
item.description: As fuel protests bring Ireland to a standstill and UK leaders decry price hikes driven by the Iran war, Europe -- barely recovered from the energy shock of the Ukraine war -- faces another crisis.
item.content:encoded: As fuel protests bring Ireland to a standstill and UK leaders decry price hikes driven by the Iran war, Europe -- barely recovered from the energy shock of the Ukraine war -- faces another crisis.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Al-Kassab
